events.title: Severe storms in China bring tornadoes and landslides that have killed 15 people
events.description: Chinese leader Xi Jinping calls for ‘all out’ rescue effort as death toll rises and 16 people ⁠remain buried after a ⁠landslide in the country’s west The death toll from devastating storms in parts of China rose to 15 on Tuesday, with hundreds more injured and tens of thousands evacuated, state media reported, as the country’s leader, Xi Jinping, urged “all out” efforts to rescue people affected by the weather. Thunderstorms and gale-force winds killed at least 11 people and injured 331 in the central province of Hubei, where “severe convective weather” hit cities, while tornadoes were reported elsewhere late Monday, state news agency Xinhua said. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Severe storms in China bring tornadoes and landslides that have killed 15 people

events.title: US-Iran conflict escalates as fresh missile strikes threaten Middle East ceasefire
events.description: The United States launched fresh air strikes on Iran early Thursday, prompting Tehran to fire missiles at its neighbours Bahrain, Qatar, Kuwait and Jordan hosting US military forces, officials said. The exchanges threatened a fragile interim ceasefire aimed at ending the Middle East conflict, with explosions also reported near Iran's nuclear power plant.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US-Iran conflict escalates as fresh missile strikes threaten Middle East ceasefire

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Open debate on sexual violence in conflict
events.description: The UN Security Council is holding an open debate Tuesday honouring the promise of international law to survivors of sexual violence in conflict as more reports emerge about warring parties using rape as a weapon, actions that constitute war crimes. Stay tuned for live updates from our meetings coverage team.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: Open debate on sexual violence in conflict

events.title: Venomous snakes escape breeding farms in southern China during flooding
events.description: Local media in Hengzhou report king ratsnakes and cobras among hundreds in flood waters caused by typhoon Maysak Hundreds of snakes, including cobras, have escaped from flooded breeding farms in southern China as severe storms continue to batter parts of the country. State media reported that a snake farm in Hengzhou, in Guangxi Zhuang autonomous region, was hit by flood waters after days of heavy rainfall caused by typhoon Maysak , prompting warnings for nearby residents. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Venomous snakes escape breeding farms in southern China during flooding

events.title: ‘Shared blueprint for peace’: Development goals deliver for billions, but challenges remain

events.title: French prosecutors investigate racist abuse of Kylian Mbappé by Paraguayan senator
events.description: Celeste Amarilla could face charges after French Football Federation complains about social media posts over World Cup match Prosecutors in France have opened an investigation into the racist attack on Kylian Mbappé by a Paraguayan senator, with officials weighing whether to demand that the senator be charged with aggravated public insult or incitement to hatred or violence. The Paris prosecutor’s office told the Guardian on Tuesday it had launched the inquiry after the French Football Federation (FFF) filed a complaint with the national unit for combating online hate. Continue reading...

events.title: Live: Iran buries Ali Khamenei after new fighting with US erupts
events.description: Iran on Friday laid to rest Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, more than four months after his death in an air strike, as two days of US and Iranian attacks raised fears of a return to all-out war. Khamenei's flag-covered coffin was carried into the shrine of Imam Reza in his home city of Mashhad in eastern Iran amid a sea of people, with no sign of a public appearance by his son and successor Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei. Follow our liveblog for all the latest updates.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Live: Iran buries Ali Khamenei after new fighting with US erupts

events.title: Srebrenica survivors warn of genocide denial while safeguarding memory of the dead

events.title: Australia news live: Telstra must ‘face the music’, minister says; SA police rule out link between death and Telstra outage
events.description: Anika Wells says telco has ‘a lot of questions to answer’. Follow updates live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Telstra aware of investigation into death in South Australia during triple zero issues Telstra said it is aware South Australia police are investigating the death of a person during the company’s network issues. SA police said yesterday they were investigating the death, which occurred at a regional hospital on Wednesday. Our thoughts are with the person’s family and loved ones at this difficult time. We are working closely with South Australia Police to support their investigation and to establish whether there is any connection between this matter and yesterday’s network outage. We are working to get rid of the Labor government, that’s our focus. Our focus isn’t on some kind of coalition or alliance. Our focus is on getting rid of a rotten Labor government. I want us to be in government in our own right. That is not our plan. Our plan is to get rid of the government. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Australia news live: Telstra must ‘face the music’, minister says; SA police rule out link between death and Telstra outage

events.title: NSW spent almost $500,000 on court cases they lost against pro-Palestine protest groups
events.description: Exclusive : Documents show $117,455 was spent in a single legal fight over laws rushed through after Bondi attack Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The New South Wales government and police have spent almost half a million dollars in legal fees on cases they lost against pro-Palestine groups, including when unsuccessfully defending constitutional challenges against protest laws expected to be struck down. The costs, outlined in documents obtained by Guardian Australia via freedom of information legislation, include $117,455.50 of taxpayers’ money on a single challenge to controversial public assembly restriction declaration (Pard) laws. Those laws were enacted after the Bondi beach terror attack and were in place during a now infamous rally against the Israeli president , Isaac Herzog, in February. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 NSW spent almost $500,000 on court cases they lost against pro-Palestine protest groups

events.title: Furore in Nigeria over fake federal agency set up in government HQ
events.description: President orders investigation after fictitious body given funding, triggering renewed scrutiny of alleged corruption A fictitious federal entity that was allocated 1.3bn naira (£700,000) in Nigeria’s 2026 budget has precipitated a political storm in Africa’s largest democracy in the run-up to a general election set for January. The fake agency came to light last October when Femi Gbajabiamila, the president’s chief of staff, wrote to the police alleging that his signature, along with official seals and reference numbers, had been forged by Adeniyi Adeyemi Matthew, who was claiming to have been appointed by the presidency to head the presidential foreign intervention promotion council (PFIPC). Continue reading...

events.title: Mexico investigates whether US lied about role in capture of drug lord
events.description: Reporting suggests FBI involved in seizure of Ismael ‘El Mayo’ Zambada García from Mexican territory in 2024 Mexico has launched an investigation into whether the US lied about its involvement in the capture and secretive transfer of a top Sinaloa cartel member in 2024, in what would be a potential violation of the country’s sovereignty. The US has long denied it played any role in the operation to detain the drug lord Ismael “El Mayo” Zambada García, a founder of the Sinaloa cartel, inside Mexico. Recent reporting by the local media outlet Pie de Nota , however, suggested that the FBI was involved in his capture. Continue reading...

events.title: FIFA World Cup: Fake racism videos go viral as players, fans face real abuse
events.description: AI fake videos, viral hoaxes and real racism are colliding at the FIFA World Cup. From a fake AI-generated Ronald Koeman rant and false claims targeting Algerian fans to the racist abuse of Kylian Mbappé and FIFA investigations, misinformation is spreading alongside genuine discrimination. Vedika Bahl fact-checks the biggest World Cup racism claims, exposes the fake ones, and separates viral AI deception from real incidents of racist abuse on and off the pitch.

events.title: ‘A lot of red flags’: plans for New Zealand’s first datacentre spark concern as locals demand greater transparency
events.description: Plans to build a NZ$3.5bn datacentre in Makarewa in the country’s south has drawn concern about electricity and water use, and potential noise pollution People living near the site of New Zealand’s first planned AI datacentre are calling for more transparency about the project, especially about how the centre’s huge electricity and water use and potential noise pollution could affect them. Singapore-based company Datagrid has secured approval to build a NZ$3.5bn (US$2bn) AI datacentre on a 49-hectare site in Makarewa, just north of New Zealand’s southern-most city, Invercargill. Construction is due to begin this year, with the centre becoming operational by 2028. Continue reading...

events.title: Ebola death toll reaches 600, as new cases suspected in other parts of Congo
events.description: New suspected cases of Ebola have been reported in parts of Congo that were previously unaffected, the government said, signaling the continued spread of the disease beyond the epicenter in Ituri.
events.ticker_label: 🚑 Ebola death toll reaches 600, as new cases suspected in other parts of Congo

events.title: Sudan: ‘El Obeid must not become the next crime scene’ warn UN investigators
events.description: Atrocities committed by the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) militia against Sudanese civilians in El Fasher in October – including mass killings, abductions and gang rapes – bear the markers of genocide, independent UN human rights investigators reported on Wednesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Sudan: ‘El Obeid must not become the next crime scene’ warn UN investigators

events.title: Critics say Turkey's verbal attacks on Israel have crossed into antisemitism
events.description: Turkish FM Hakan Fidan reportedly called Israel a burden humanity can no longer bear, drawing accusations of genocidal rhetoric as Turkey hosts a NATO summit in Ankara.

events.title: Justin Bieber joins Madonna, Shakira and BTS for Fifa World Cup final half-time show
events.description: The Super Bowl-style show marks the Canadian singer's latest big performance after his comeback shows at Coachella.

events.title: ‘Unstoppable force’: Mbappe’s France crush Morocco’s World Cup dreams
events.description: Morocco came to the quarterfinal with high hopes and revenge on their minds, only to be met with the in-form team.

events.title: Spain vs Belgium: World Cup quarterfinal – prediction, start time, lineups
events.description: Spain, aiming to reach the semifinals for the first time since 2010, face Belgium in Friday's contest in Los Angeles.

events.title: Norway change World Cup team hotel in Miami days before England showdown
events.description: The team spent just one night at their hotel before deciding to leave due to excessive construction noise.

events.title: How World Bank and IMF loans are reshaping policymaking in Africa
events.description: As debt pressures grow, African governments are reassessing the trade-offs of concessional financing.

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: Catnip lotion as effective as Deet at repelling mosquitoes, study finds
events.description: Researchers testing a cheap, homegrown oil in Uganda found what cats knew all along – it worked as well as the artificial chemical used globally A homegrown catnip lotion has proven “just as effective as Deet” as a mosquito repellant in trials carried out in Uganda. Catnip, or Nepeta cataria , is a common herb from the mint family. The chemical in the plant that causes feline euphoria – nepetalactone – also has insect-repelling properties but this has not previously been commercialised. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Catnip lotion as effective as Deet at repelling mosquitoes, study finds

events.title: Egypt complains officials were biased in World Cup loss to Argentina
events.description: The Egyptian Football Association (EFA) said Wednesday it "cannot remain silent" after what it believes was unfair and biased officiating in Egypt's 3-2 round of 16 loss against Argentina on Tuesday.

events.title: UK: Police attempt to remove loyalist bonfire 'hate display'
events.description: A loyalist bonfire in Northern Ireland was lit a day early before police could remove a replica mosque from its top. Political and religious leaders had condemned what organizers insisted was a "political protest."

events.title: Syria regains voting rights at world chemical weapons watchdog
events.description: The Organization for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons (OPCW) cited a "change in circumstances" as the reason behind its decision. Syria had joined the watchdog in 2013, only to be stripped of its rights in 2021.

events.title: France book World Cup semi-final place with win over Morocco thanks to Mbappé, Dembélé
events.description: Les Bleus advanced to the World Cup semi-finals for the third consecutive time on Thursday. Didier Deschamps' side struggled for the first 45 minutes against Morocco’s defence before making the breakthrough in the second half thanks to Kylian Mbappé and Ousmane Dembélé (2-0). They will face Spain or Belgium for a spot in the final.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Moroccan fans meet to watch the quarter-final against France in Boston
events.description: Boston is home to a large Moroccan community, one of the largest in the US. And they were out in force for the quarter-final against France.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Mbappé clears air around penalty against Morocco
events.description: Though he found the back of the net later in the match, France captain Kylian Mbappé criticised himself for losing focus and not scoring from the penalty spot in the first half against Morocco. When asked whether the 2026 French squad was the strongest French side he had seen, Mbappé was quick not to let the team rest on their laurels of going unbeaten in the 2026 edition of the FIFA World Cup.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Mbappé finds the answer as France advance past Morocco
events.description: No description available.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Mbappé's breakthrough sends France past Morocco to semi-finals
events.description: France booked their place in the semi-finals for the third time in a row with a 2-0 victory on Thursday over Morocco, overcoming an early penalty miss to break down the resilient Atlas Lions. Kylian Mbappé's breakthrough goal and Ballon d'Or holder Ousmane Dembélé's finish proved decisive as the Bleus progressed to the World Cup's final four.

events.title: World Cup: ATFrenchies share their France-Morocco predictions
events.description: ⚽ 🏆 Alexandre Durand and Tom Carles, the duo that make up AT Frenchies, are showing their support for France's #LesBleus as they face #Morocco in the first quarter final at the FIFA World Cup. 🇫🇷 🇲🇦 Who do they think will win? They share their analysis with FRANCE 24 ▶️

events.title: Nike and Adidas locked in World Cup standoff as tournament heats up

events.title: Protests threaten to spiral out of control after rape and murder of girl, 12, in India
events.description: Violent protests are threating to spiral out of control in India after the rape and murder of a 12-year-old girl.

events.title: Energy costs rise and stocks fall sharply as US-Iran peace is shattered
events.description: There has been a marked increase in global energy prices amid fears of a prolonged resumption to hostilities between the US and Iran.

events.title: World must move faster on Ebola response for DR Congo: UN relief chief
events.description: The world must move faster to contain the spread of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), UN relief chief Tom Fletcher said on Thursday.

events.title: Remembering the Srebrenica genocide: ‘Fear is all that kept me moving’
events.description: The United Nations paused on Wednesday to remember the more than 8,000 Bosnian Muslim men and boys killed in the town of Srebrenica in July 1995, and the women and survivors left to rebuild their lives in the aftermath.

events.title: Peace hinges on people-centred policing, global summit hears
events.description: Policing is the cornerstone of peace, said top UN officials ahead of the fifth UN Chiefs of Police Summit, which began on Tuesday.

events.title: Tens of thousands of far-left protesters clash with police in anti-conservative party riots
events.description: Videos show police deploying batons and anti-riot measures as over 30,000 far-left protesters clashed with officers outside AfD's party conference.

events.title: World leaders, dignitaries pay tribute to America on historic 250th birthday
events.description: King Charles and Pope Leo XIV were among world leaders sending messages to Trump on America's 250th Independence Day. From Jerusalem to the UAE and Japan congratulations were sent.

events.title: Britain’s likely PM says will work to ‘stop the suffering’ in Gaza

events.description: A fire has broken out at a shoe factory in China's Fujian province, killing 28 people

events.title: Restoring dignity: Senegalese surgeon and Burundian foundation win reproductive health awards
events.description: A Senegalese surgeon who has dedicated his career to restoring the health and dignity of women living with obstetric fistula, and a Burundian foundation supporting vulnerable women and children, have been named the winners of the 2026 United Nations Population Award.

events.description: A North Carolina man allegedly broke into his ex-girlfriend's apartment and stabbed his 5-year-old son before being arrested at a nearby Taco Bell.

events.title: Deadly H5 bird flu found in local Australian seabird for first time
events.description: Native greater crested tern – a common coastal bird – tests positive for disease after being discovered at Robe on SA’s Limestone Coast Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The first case of deadly H5 bird flu in local wildlife has been recorded in a bird found on the South Australian coast, with experts saying the discovery is an escalation of the disease’s arrival in the country. Separately, in New South Wales, a young fur seal found at Blue Bay on the Central Coast was being tested for H5 bird flu as a precaution. The animal died on Thursday. Guardian Australia understands that tests were negative for bird flu. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Deadly H5 bird flu found in local Australian seabird for first time

events.title: How England's class divide shaped Andy Burnham, the U.K.'s likely next prime minister
events.description: As mayor of Manchester, Andy Burnham brought growth to the postindustrial city. Can he scale that nationally as the next prime minister?

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
events.description: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

events.title: UK judges begin hearing appeal over Trinidad and Tobago anti-gay law
events.description: Activist is challenging ruling last year that restored colonial-era homophobic law against same-sex intimacy Some of the UK’s top judges are hearing arguments over whether a Trinidad and Tobago court had the legal right to overturn a 2018 ruling to remove colonial-era homophobic laws that criminalise anal sex between consenting men. The country’s “ buggery law ”, often referred to as its “sodomy” law, was created in 1925 and was written into Trinidad and Tobago’s 1986 Sexual Offences Act . In 2017 a Trinidadian LGBTQ+ rights activist, Jason Jones , challenged the law, and in 2018 a high court ruled that it infringed upon his constitutional right to privacy and equality. Continue reading...

events.title: India news: Prime Minister Modi embarks on maiden trip to New Zealand
events.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi is concluding a tri-country tour with a visit to New Zealand. On his docket are biltaeral talks on trade, commerce and defense. DW has more.

events.title: S. Korea's Supreme Court upholds prison sentence for Yoon in first martial law case
events.description: South Korea’s Supreme Court has upheld a seven-year prison sentence for former President Yoon Suk Yeol in the first case to reach the country’s highest court from the several criminal trials related to his brief imposition of martial law in 2024

events.title: A bitterly divided Iran grapples with Khamenei's legacy as he is laid to rest

events.title: Species’ ingenious survival strategies no match for human destruction, red list reveals
events.description: Newly endangered animals include desert frogs and snails in extreme ocean depths, both threatened by mining Life has colonised every corner of the planet by evolving ingenious survival strategies but these are increasingly being overwhelmed by destructive human activities, this year’s red list of endangered species has revealed. Many snails, limpets and clams have adapted to life at crushing depths in the oceans on hydrothermal vents where water temperatures can reach 450C (842F). But an assessment for the red list found that two-thirds of the hundreds of mollusc species found only on deep sea vents were at risk of extinction because of deep-sea mining . Continue reading...

events.title: Trump wraps NATO summit on a positive note,  after meeting Zelenskyy
events.description: President Trump capped a NATO summit in Turkey meeting Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, and saying that the U.S. will give Ukraine a license to make Patriot air defense systems.

events.title: South Africa: Driven home by anti-migrant protests

events.title: Nigel Farage resigning from UK Parliament
events.description: Reform UK leader Nigel Farage has resigned from Parliament to force a snap by-election following an ongoing parliamentary investigation into a €5 million gift from a crypto billionaire, which Farage defended as necessary funding for his personal security.

events.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand

events.title: Iran had 'specific' plan to kill Trump, US media report citing Israeli intel
events.description: Iran planned to kill US President Donald Trump in a fresh plot unveiled by Israel, several US media outlets report. According to Israeli intelligence, the plan was “specific” and may explain why Trump chose to switch planes when leaving the NATO summit in Turkey earlier this week.

events.title: Bayeux Tapestry arrives safely in UK after loan from France
events.description: The medieval Bayeux Tapestry arrived in London after almost 1,000 years early on Friday. The tapestry is on loan from France for a year and will be exhibited at the British Museum. Its journey has been surrounded by secrecy due to security concerns.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Abuse survivors launch bid to transfer claims to entity that benefited from Christian Brothers’ wealth

events.title: Germany: Bundestag to vote on health care savings
events.description: The cost-cutting package is expected to pass Germany's lower house. Elsewhere, Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul will meet Slovenian counterpart Tone Kajzer in Berlin. Follow DW for more.

events.title: Bayeux Tapestry arrives in British Museum for historic exhibition
events.description: After nearly 1,000 years, the medieval masterpiece is back on British soil. The Bayeux Tapestry was transferred from France to London in a highly secretive operation and will be on display in September.

events.title: US: Charlie Kirk murder suspect 'regretted' act, roommate says
events.description: Prosecutors in the Charlie Kirk murder case released details from an interview with the suspect's roommate, where he allegedly admitted to the murder and that he regretted it.

events.title: EU lawmakers pave way for 'digital euro' negotiations
events.description: The digital euro has been touted as the EU's best chance at breaking its dependence on US payment systems like Visa and Mastercard, as well as Apple Pay and Google Pay.

events.title: Ukraine denies involvement in Nord Stream blasts
events.description: Ukraine's prosecutor general proposed forming a joint investigative team with Germany. Berlin has indicted a former Ukrainian soldier over the blasts and says he was working on behalf of Ukrainian entities.

events.title: Abbas sets Palestinian legislative elections for November
events.description: The Palestinian Authority has announced it will soon hold its first elections in twenty years. However, questions remain about whether it has the means and legitimacy to do so.

events.title: How do Germans cope with having Nazi grandparents?
events.description: Since millions of NSDAP membership cards have been made available online, many Germans have been surprised to discover that their ancestors weren't always as innocent as family stories had led them to believe.

events.title: Congo: UN laments uptick in fighting in South Kivu
events.description: The UN human rights chief has called for an immediate halt to fighting in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo. This follows intensified clashses between the army and the Rwanda-backed M23 militia in South Kivu.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Congo: UN laments uptick in fighting in South Kivu

events.title: Iran after Khamenei: A new order takes shape
events.description: Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran's new supreme leader, has inherited a political system that his father spent decades building and shaping in his own image.

events.title: 🔴Bayeux Tapestry arrives safely in UK after loan from France
events.description: No description available.

events.title: Woman forced to bury husband in Cape Verde after he fell ill and died while on holiday
events.description: A British woman said she was forced to bury her husband in an unmarked grave after he fell ill and died in Cape Verde.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Woman forced to bury husband in Cape Verde after he fell ill and died while on holiday

events.title: Florida airport renamed after Donald Trump
events.description: An airport in South Florida has officially been renamed after Donald Trump - the first time an airport has been named after a sitting US president.

events.title: Australia agrees to sell uranium to India, ending a long stalemate
events.description: Australia has agreed to sell uranium to India for peaceful purposes

events.title: Nominations open in the contest to be UK leader, Burnham likely the only candidate
events.description: Nominations are opening in a Labour Party election to replace Keir Starmer as Britain's prime minister

events.title: An Italian region defies US pressure to end a Cuban doctors program
events.description: Cuban doctors are working in hospitals in Italy's Calabria region under a long-running program despite U.S. efforts to end it

events.title: Africa secures $900 million in new clean cooking commitments
events.description: African countries have secured $900 million in new financial commitments to expand access to clean cooking

events.title: Diplomats go virtual to witness Gaza displacement site up close
events.description: Representatives from 12 countries carried out a “virtual diplomatic field visit” to a displacement site in the Gaza Strip and heard from some of the residents about their pressing needs, the United Nations said on Thursday.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Diplomats go virtual to witness Gaza displacement site up close

events.title: Florida man who contacted police about 1987 killing arrested in connection to cold case
events.description: A 70-year-old man who called Jacksonville Sheriff's Office claiming to have information about a 1987 killing was arrested and charged with murder.

events.title: Black bear raiding a garbage can at Lake Tahoe garage triggers dramatic wildlife encounter
events.description: Video captured the moment a black bear charged a Sacramento man outside his Lake Tahoe vacation home, forcing him to deploy bear spray nearby.

events.title: Accused Charlie Kirk assassin allegedly confessed in texts, apologized and described motive, agent testifies
events.description: Text messages read in court reveal that Tyler Robinson allegedly told his roommate and lover that he shot Charlie Kirk because he "had enough of his hatred."

events.title: Illegal immigrant soccer coach who used alcohol and drugs to sexually abuse kids learns fate
events.description: A tipster discovered explicit videos on a cellphone left at a Franklin, Tennessee, business, launching a probe into the illegal immigrant coach.

events.title: Former cop Derek Chauvin appeals judge's rejection of bid for new trial in George Floyd murder case
events.description: Derek Chauvin filed an appeal after a judge denied his post-conviction relief petition challenging jury instructions and video evidence in the Floyd case.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Former cop Derek Chauvin appeals judge's rejection of bid for new trial in George Floyd murder case

events.title: Family of toddler found alive in morgue after being declared dead plans legal action
events.description: Bodycam footage shows a doctor declaring 1-year-old Vincent Fiordilino dead, but signs of life were detected minutes later by a nurse and mother.

events.title: Air Force rescinding 135 promotions after test scoring error: 'This is going to be hard'
events.description: An outdated scoring key used on the specialty knowledge test forced the Air Force to revoke 135 technical sergeant promotions in security forces.

events.title: Charlie Kirk family demands judge reveal hidden evidence from accused assassin's hearing
events.description: Erika Kirk's court filing argues victims have a right to view evidence presented during Tyler Robinson's preliminary hearing for Kirk's assassination.

events.title: Minnesota yanks race-based grant after DOJ initiated Civil Rights review, DOJ reveals
events.description: The DOJ says Minnesota's Department of Health suspended a race-based grant program after a Title VI civil rights review found it prioritized by race.

events.title: US urges donors to abandon UNRWA funding as UN defends agency's mission
events.description: U.S. Ambassador Jeff Bartos accused UNRWA of becoming a "subsidiary of Hamas" and urged donors to fund the Board of Peace instead at the conference.

events.title: Conservative Keiko Fujimori officially declared winner of Peru's presidential runoff election
events.description: Keiko Fujimori won Peru's presidential runoff after defeating Roberto Sánchez by a razor-thin margin, pledging an "iron fist" against surging crime.
